title: Alaticaulia posadae
taxonomy: Alaticaulia posadae is classified in the family Amaryllidaceae. This classification places it within the order Asparagales. At the class level, Alaticaulia posadae is classified as Liliopsida. It belongs to the division Magnoliophyta and the kingdom Plantae. The family Amaryllidaceae is a large group of flowering plants commonly known as the amaryllis family.

intro: Alaticaulia posadae, also known as Alatocaule or Posada's Alatocaule, is a plant species in the family Boraginaceae, native to Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of various infections. The bioactive constituents of Alaticaulia posadae include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Alatocaule is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat respiratory issues, gastrointestinal problems, and skin conditions.
